examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

info.description: A lightweight, self-hosted Docker Compose management platform with GitOps, pipelines, and RBAC.
info.title: Composer

get.description: Returns the most recent audit entries, newest first. Use `action`/`stack` to filter. Entries older than 30 days are automatically pruned. Admin only.

parameters.description: Filter by stack name (matches in resource path)
parameters.name: stack

schema.description: Filter by stack name (matches in resource path)

get.description: Returns {needed: true} while the user table is empty. Used by the setup wizard to decide whether to prompt for first-admin creation. Public endpoint.
get.summary: Check if bootstrap is needed (no users exist)

post.description: Creates the initial admin account. Rejected with 409 once any user exists. Public endpoint (this is the only way to escape the chicken-and-egg auth requirement).
Authenticates against the user table and issues a session. Sets the `composer_session` cookie on success. Rate-limited per email and per IP. Public endpoint.
post.summary: Log in with email and password

post.description: Revokes the current session token and clears the `composer_session` cookie. Requires an active session.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's ID and role when the session or API key is valid. Used by SPAs to populate the current-user UI state.

post.description: Deletes BuildKit cache. Admin only.

get.description: Returns every container visible to the Docker daemon, including non-compose ones. For stack-scoped views call `getStack` instead.

post.description: Deletes all stopped containers on the daemon. Admin only.

get.description: Returns a single container's state, image, service, health, and restart policy.
Returns a one-shot log snapshot. For live streaming use the SSE endpoint at `/api/v1/sse/containers/{id}/logs`. Output is capped at 1MB.

parameters.description: Number of lines from the end ('all' for full history)
parameters.name: tail

schema.description: Number of lines from the end ('all' for full history)

parameters.description: Show logs since RFC3339 timestamp or Go duration (e.g. 5m, 2h)
parameters.name: since

schema.description: Show logs since RFC3339 timestamp or Go duration (e.g. 5m, 2h)

post.description: Stops then starts a container in one call. Compose-managed only.
Starts a stopped container. Restricted to compose-managed containers (has `com.docker.compose.project` label) to avoid operating on infrastructure containers.
Gracefully stops a running container (SIGTERM then SIGKILL after 10s). Compose-managed only.

get.description: Returns Docker daemon events from the last `since` duration (default 5 minutes). Caps at 100 events to avoid unbounded responses.

parameters.description: How far back to look, as Go duration (e.g. 5m, 1h, 30m)
parameters.name: since

schema.description: How far back to look, as Go duration (e.g. 5m, 1h, 30m)

post.description: Executes a whitelisted `docker <subcommand>` on the host. Intended as an SSH-free debugging console. Allowlisted to read-only subcommands (ps, images, network, volume, system, info, version, inspect, logs, stats, top, port, diff, history, search, tag). Admin only.

Equivalent to `docker system prune`. Removes stopped containers, unused networks, dangling images, and build cache. Pass `?all=true` for all unused images, `?volumes=true` to also prune volumes. Admin only.

parameters.description: Remove all unused images (not just dangling)
parameters.name: all

schema.description: Remove all unused images (not just dangling)

get.description: Returns all local Docker images with tags, sizes, and creation timestamps.

post.description: Deletes dangling images. Pass `?all=true` to also delete unused tagged images. Admin only.

parameters.description: Remove all unused images, not just dangling/untagged
parameters.name: all

schema.description: Remove all unused images, not just dangling/untagged

post.description: Pulls an image from its registry. Accepts any valid image ref (nginx, nginx:alpine, ghcr.io/owner/repo:tag).

delete.description: Deletes the image. Fails if any container (including stopped) uses it.

get.description: Returns up to 100 background jobs, newest first. Jobs are created by async compose operations (?async=true) and webhook-triggered GitOps syncs. Completed/failed jobs are pruned after 1 hour.
Returns the status, captured output, and timestamps for a single job. Poll this endpoint to track async compose operations.
Returns every API key's ID, name, role, last-used and expiry timestamps. Plaintext values are never returned here — they're only shown once at creation.

post.description: Generates a new API key with the given name and role. The plaintext key is returned ONCE in the response body — save it immediately. Operators can only create keys at their role level or below.

delete.description: Permanently revokes the API key. Requests using the key will immediately start returning 401.

get.description: Returns metadata about a single API key (no plaintext).
Returns all Docker networks visible to the daemon including the default bridge and compose-created networks.

post.description: Creates a new network with the specified driver. Defaults to `bridge` driver when omitted.
Deletes networks with no active endpoints. Admin only.

delete.description: Deletes the network. Fails if the network has active endpoints (detach containers first).

get.description: Returns detailed network configuration (IPAM, attached containers, options, labels).
Returns every pipeline with step count and creation metadata. Operator+.

post.description: Creates a new pipeline with steps and triggers. Admin only — pipelines can execute shell commands and docker operations on the host.

delete.description: Deletes a pipeline and its run history. Active runs continue until they finish. Operator+.

get.description: Returns the full pipeline definition including steps, triggers, and timestamps. Operator+.

put.description: Replaces the pipeline definition. When the updated steps include `shell_command` or `docker_exec` types the caller must be admin (prevents privilege escalation via pipeline edit).

post.description: Cancels the most recent pending or running run of this pipeline by cancelling its executor context. Completed runs are left untouched.
Starts a new pipeline run asynchronously. Returns the run ID immediately; poll `getPipelineRun` or stream `/api/v1/sse/pipelines/{id}/runs/{runId}` for status.

get.description: Returns a pipeline's run history, newest first. Operator+.
Returns the run status plus step-by-step results (output, duration, error).
SSE stream of log lines from a single container. Demuxes Docker's stdout/stderr frames and strips ANSI escapes. Viewer+.

parameters.description: Number of lines from the end
parameters.name: tail

schema.description: Number of lines from the end

parameters.description: Show logs since RFC3339 timestamp or Go duration
parameters.name: since

schema.description: Show logs since RFC3339 timestamp or Go duration
Each oneOf object in the array represents one possible Server Sent Events (SSE) message, serialized as UTF-8 text according to the SSE specification.
schema.title: Server Sent Events

get.description: SSE stream of container resource usage (~1 event/sec): CPU%, memory, network, block I/O, PID count. Viewer+.

schema.description: Each oneOf object in the array represents one possible Server Sent Events (SSE) message, serialized as UTF-8 text according to the SSE specification.
schema.title: Server Sent Events

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream of domain events (stack lifecycle, container state/health). Clients should reconnect on disconnection; events are fire-and-forget and not replayed. Viewer+.

schema.description: Each oneOf object in the array represents one possible Server Sent Events (SSE) message, serialized as UTF-8 text according to the SSE specification.
schema.title: Server Sent Events

get.description: SSE stream filtered to a specific pipeline run. Emits `pipeline.step.started`, `pipeline.step.finished`, and `pipeline.run.finished` events. The stream auto-closes when the run finishes. Operator+.

schema.description: Each oneOf object in the array represents one possible Server Sent Events (SSE) message, serialized as UTF-8 text according to the SSE specification.
schema.title: Server Sent Events

get.description: SSE stream that multiplexes logs from every container in the stack. Each event is prefixed with the container name. Viewer+.

schema.description: Each oneOf object in the array represents one possible Server Sent Events (SSE) message, serialized as UTF-8 text according to the SSE specification.
schema.title: Server Sent Events

get.summary: Stream aggregated logs for all services in a stack

get.description: Returns every stack known to Composer with container counts and status (running / stopped / partial / unknown). Viewer+.

post.description: Creates a local-source stack from the supplied compose YAML. The compose content is written to `<stacks_dir>/<name>/compose.yaml` but not deployed. Use `deployStack` to bring services up.

Clones the given repo (HTTPS or SSH) into a new stack directory and registers it as git-backed. Credentials may be passed inline or resolved from global settings. The stack is created but not deployed.
post.summary: Clone a git repo and create a git-backed stack

post.description: Scans a host directory (e.g. a Dockge data dir) and imports any discovered compose projects as local stacks. Existing stacks are skipped. Admin only.
post.summary: Import stacks from an external directory

delete.description: Stops the stack, removes containers, and deletes the stack directory. Pass `?remove_volumes=true` to also drop named volumes (destructive).

get.description: Returns compose content, `.env` content (optionally SOPS-decrypted), detected Dockerfiles, containers, and git config (if git-backed).

put.description: Overwrites the compose.yaml on disk. For git-backed stacks this marks sync_status=dirty until a new commit is pushed or rollback is performed.

post.description: Runs `docker compose up -d --build` to rebuild images from Dockerfiles then deploy. Supports `?async=true`.

parameters.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking
parameters.name: async

schema.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking

post.description: Turns an existing local stack into a git-tracked stack by cloning the provided repo over the stack directory. Credentials can be supplied per-stack or defer to global settings.
post.summary: Convert a local stack to git-backed

post.description: Removes the stack's git metadata so it's no longer tracked against a remote. The current on-disk compose content is preserved; future edits happen locally only.
post.summary: Detach a git-backed stack and convert to local

get.description: Returns the effective git/SSH/SOPS credentials used for this stack plus where each came from (per-stack, global, env). Redacts secret material — only sources are reported.
get.summary: Get resolved credential chain for a stack

put.description: Replaces the stack's per-stack credentials (git token, SSH key, SOPS age key, basic-auth). Sending an empty value for a field clears that override so the global credential takes effect.

post.description: Full GitOps pipeline: `git pull` → SOPS decrypt `.env` → `docker compose pull` → `docker compose up -d` → re-encrypt. Designed for CI to call after image push. Use `?async=true` for background execution.

parameters.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking
parameters.name: async

schema.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking

post.summary: Sync git, pull images, and redeploy

get.description: Computes a line diff between the on-disk compose.yaml and Docker's resolved config. Useful to preview what a deploy would actually apply.
get.summary: Show pending compose changes vs saved version

post.description: Runs `docker compose down` to stop and remove containers. Volumes and networks are preserved. Supports `?async=true`.

parameters.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking
parameters.name: async

schema.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking

put.description: Writes the provided content to `<stack>/.env`. Sending empty content deletes the file. Does not validate content or trigger a deploy.

post.description: Runs an allowlisted `docker compose <args>` in the stack's directory. Read-only subcommands (ps, logs, top, config, images, port, version, ls, events, build) are operator+. Shell-equivalent subcommands (exec, cp) require admin.
post.summary: Run a docker compose command against this stack

get.description: Returns a line-by-line diff of the working tree against git HEAD. Useful to see local edits before syncing.
Returns commits on the tracked branch with a flag indicating whether each commit touched the compose file. Use `?limit=N` to cap (default 20, max 100).
Returns repo URL, tracked branch, last sync timestamp, last-synced commit SHA, and the sync status (synced/behind/diverged/dirty/error/syncing).

post.description: Pulls the newest version of every image referenced by compose.yaml. Does not redeploy — call `deployStack` afterwards to apply. Supports `?async=true`.

parameters.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking
parameters.name: async

schema.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking

post.description: Restarts all services in the stack via `docker compose restart`. Supports `?async=true`.

parameters.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking
parameters.name: async

schema.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking

post.description: Resets the working tree to the given commit SHA. Equivalent to `git checkout <sha>`. Does not redeploy — call deploy after to apply.
Fast-forwards the stack to the latest commit on its tracked branch and reports whether the compose file changed. Clears the dirty flag on success.
Runs `docker compose up -d` in the stack directory. Use `?async=true` to return a job ID immediately and poll `/api/v1/jobs/{id}` for completion. Synchronous calls time out at 10 minutes.

parameters.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking
parameters.name: async

schema.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking

post.description: Runs `docker compose config` to validate and normalize the compose file. Returns the validation output; non-zero exit surfaces compose syntax errors.

parameters.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking
parameters.name: async

schema.description: Run asynchronously and return a job ID instead of blocking

get.description: Reports SSH keys, SOPS age key, global git token, encryption key source, and database type. Secret material is always redacted. Admin only.

put.description: Stores a provided age private key as the global SOPS decryption key. Send empty string to remove. Admin only.
put.summary: Set or update the global age key for SOPS

post.description: Generates and persists a fresh age keypair for SOPS. The private key is returned ONCE in the response; save or encrypt it externally. Admin only.
post.summary: Generate a new age key pair and save

get.description: Returns whether a global git token is configured and a preview (first 8 chars). Admin only.

put.description: Stores a plaintext git token encrypted at rest. Empty value removes the token. Admin only.
put.summary: Set or remove global git access token

post.description: Writes a PEM-encoded SSH private key to the composer SSH dir and encrypts it at rest. Admin only.
post.summary: Add an SSH key by pasting content

delete.description: Removes the SSH key file (and its .pub counterpart if present). Admin only.

get.description: Public liveness probe. Returns {status, version}. No authentication required.
Returns Docker daemon version, runtime, OS/arch, and counts of containers and images. Requires Docker to be available.
Returns Composer version, Go runtime version, OS/arch, and process uptime. Viewer+.
Returns the built-in stack template catalog (nginx, postgres, etc.). Public endpoint to help onboarding before login.
Returns the full compose.yaml body for a template. Public endpoint.
Returns every user account with role and last-login timestamp. Admin only.

post.description: Creates a local user with email + bcrypt-hashed password and assigns a role (admin / operator / viewer). Admin only.

delete.description: Deletes the user and cascades their sessions and API keys. Admin only.

get.description: Returns a user's public profile. Admin only.

put.description: Updates a user's email and/or role. Changing role revokes all existing sessions so the new role takes effect on next login. Admin only.
Admins can change any user's password; non-admins can only change their own (and must supply the old password). Successful rotation invalidates all active sessions for the user.

get.description: Returns all named Docker volumes on the daemon.

post.description: Creates a new named volume. Defaults to `local` driver.
Deletes all volumes not referenced by any container. Destructive: data in unused volumes is permanently lost. Admin only.

delete.description: Deletes the volume and all its data. Fails if any container still references the volume.

get.description: Returns full volume metadata (driver, mountpoint, options, labels, creation time).
Returns every configured webhook (secret redacted). Operator+.

post.description: Creates a webhook receiver URL and HMAC secret for a stack. The plaintext secret is returned ONCE and must be configured in your git provider; subsequent reads return the redacted form.
post.summary: Create a webhook for a stack

delete.description: Removes the webhook. Subsequent deliveries to its URL will 404.

get.description: Returns webhook metadata with a redacted secret (last 4 chars only).

put.description: Updates branch filter, auto-redeploy flag, and/or provider. Use pointer fields to distinguish 'keep current' (omit) from 'clear' (empty string).

get.description: Returns up to 50 recent deliveries for this webhook (status, event, branch, commit, action taken). Older entries are pruned after 30 days.

tags.description: Server info, version, and global configuration
tags.name: system

tags.description: API key management (plaintext shown once at creation)
tags.name: keys

tags.description: Git-backed stack sync, rollback, and deploy pipeline
tags.name: git

tags.description: Inbound git webhook configuration and delivery history
tags.name: webhooks

tags.description: Background job status for async compose operations
tags.name: jobs

tags.description: Audit log of mutating operations (admin only)
tags.name: audit

tags.description: Server-Sent Events streams (logs, stats, events, pipeline output)
tags.name: sse
